data.headline: Gemini CLI @ Google
data.summary:
I am a software engineer with near a decade of experience and a passion for building tools and experiences for developers. I led development of some features for GitHub Copilot offerings for VS and VS Code IDEs. I was a long-time contributor to Visual Studio core, shipped code in VS Mac, and worked on a number of incubation projects. I have a uniquely broad exposure to various developer technologies at Microsoft, GitHub, and Copilot in particular. I have also made minor contributions to Azure DevOps, GitHub Code Search, and the language server protocol and LSIF specifications and clients.

Recently I've been working within GitHub Inc. on rapidly building out new scenarios for the GitHub Copilot coding agent, improving its toolset, teaching it new skills, evaluating it on its performance in various scenarios with different models, and partnering with GitHub and Microsoft engineers to integrate these experiences into GitHub.com and Microsoft IDEs.

Prior to my move to working on AI agents, I had broad exposure to client, server, data architecture, and search in various developer facing Microsoft products:
- I led the port of Next Edit Suggestions from VS Code to Copilot in VS.
- I led the building of several prototypes exposing various developer tools to Copilot.
- I led small crews in building and running RAG, context, and quality A/B experiments in GitHub Copilot Chat and Completions.
- I designed and led the deployment of a data architecture and library for a semantic code search service.
- I designed the VisualStudio.Extensibility family of API surfaces for the VS Editor, inspired by VS Code's extension host.
- I built a plain-text indexing service for accelerating search in Visual Studio for large enterprise customers.
- I ported the Language Server protocol client for VS, to VS Mac and wrote the Xamarin.Mac Cocoa UI for it.
- I built numerous UI, API, and user facing editor and shell features for Visual Studio in WPF, Win32, .NET, C++, and COM over several years.

I consider myself a technical and adaptable generalist but with significant depth in programming languages, systems, performance, search, and developer tools and experiences. I adapt quickly to new codebases, languages, and domains and am familiar with a breadth of technologies and problems.

I have done extensive TS, NodeJS, C#, C/C++, Rust, Go, Sql, Kusto, PowerShell, and many others and have worked in both Microsoft and GitHub environments and systems with Windows, Mac OS, and Linux.

experience.title: Principal Software Engineer
experience.description:
I am on the books as a Microsoft FTE but I work full time at GitHub Inc on the core GitHub Copilot coding agent runtime team. This team owns the model clients, tools, prompts, MCP client, agent feature set, and CLIs for both the hosted Coding agent and the GitHub Copilot CLI previews.

This team benchmarks models, writes agents and the core agentic framework for the experiences, and works across the CLI, agent orchestration backend service, and GitHub.com front end.

experience.title: Principal Software Engineer
experience.description:
I am working with teammates and partners across Microsoft and GitHub on integration, performance, and response quality of GitHub Copilot in various IDEs.

My primary focus is Copilot in Visual Studio, though I contribute to common client components used by the entire family of Copilot supported IDEs, VS Copilot Chat, and I have prototyped changes in GitHub services.

I specifically:
- Led the port of Next Edit Suggestions context and prompting code to a common component where it can be used for Copilot for VS and other IDEs and drove quality for the VS feature end to end: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/releases/2022/release-notes-preview?tabs=allfeatures

- Designed and led the implementation of two different foundational context indexing, retrieval, and storage components used by multiple teams across Microsoft and GitHub for their contextualization A/B experiments in Copilot for VS, Copilot for VS Code, and Copilot for IntelliJ.

- Implemented and led the rollout of A/B experiments for testing prompt changes impact on key metrics.

- Led efforts to improve Code Completions availability in Visual Studio. This consisted of deep telemetry and code analysis across the VS, Copilot, and Copilot services codebases, adding instrumentation, and creating and leading the execution of a plan to improve consistency and volume of Code Completions in VS, and resulted in significant measurable and user visible reliability improvement.

- Mentored new teammates and partners in onboarding to VS, VS Code, and GitHub repos and navigating a complex inter-company and polyglot initiative.

- Acted as a source of technical expertise with Visual Studio, VS Code, .NET, indexing, parser generators, testing, reliability, performance, and prompt crafting for dozens of developers.

- Worked closely with a blended group of Microsoft and GitHub engineers to set the direction of Copilot Completions improvements.

Currently, I lead a distributed organization of high-performing engineers and managers, driving the technical strategy for large-scale LLM application, developer experience (DevX), and cloud infrastructure. data.headline: Software Engineer III - Gemini CLI @ Google
data.summary:
As a Software Engineer III at Google, I contribute to the development of AI-powered developer tools and scalable cloud platforms. My work focuses on improving developer productivity through high-performance and intuitive software solutions that address complex challenges.  

I hold a Bachelor of Science in Software Engineering from Washington State University. My recent efforts include enhancing Gemini's response latency and developing scalable backend services like the Gemini Entity Service. I am committed to advancing innovation at the intersection of AI and developer experience.

experience.title: Software Engineer III - Gemini Code Assist
experience.description: I spearheaded several high-impact engineering initiatives to enhance developer productivity and system performance. I drove the Streaming Chat project to reduce response latency from minutes to seconds, and developed the Transform Code and Context Management features, which grew to serve tens of thousands of monthly users. Architecturally, I designed the scalable Gemini Entity Service to unify command and agent integrations for partner teams. My work also included significant performance optimization, where I identified a core inefficiency that cut QPS by 30%, yielding significant annual cost savings. In addition, I led an external workforce team, guiding their efforts to enhance product stability by prioritizing impactful bug fixes and increasing unit test coverage.

data.headline: Builder, Learner, Distinguished Engineer at Google DeepMind
data.summary:
For over 20 years, my career has been a journey of exploration, driven by a desire to learn and build impactful technology. I've been fortunate to move between leadership and hands-on technical roles, each time seeking the challenge that would teach me the most.

After years of leading large engineering organizations in Google Maps, Core, and Cloud AI, I've come full circle, returning to my roots as a builder. Today, as a Distinguished Engineer at Google DeepMind, I am focused on creating the next generation of AI agents and developer tools, including the Gemini CLI. For a builder, there is no more exciting place to be.

experience.title: Distinguished Engineer, Google DeepMind
experience.description: Returning to a full-time individual contributor role to focus on the future of AI-powered software development. My work centers on building intelligent agents and developer tools that empower builders to collaborate with AI in new and transformative ways. Core contributor to the Gemini CLI project.

experience.title: Senior Director AI Developer
experience.description: Led the teams responsible for some of Google's key AI developer products, including Kaggle, Colab, and the Gemini API & AI Studio. This role provided a unique opportunity to focus on a smaller organization with a mature leadership team, which allowed me to dedicate more time to hands-on building and contributing to both open-source and internal AI projects. My work on Gemini CLI during this time solidified my passion for building the next generation of developer tools.

experience.title: Senior Director of Engineering Core ML
experience.description: Helped form the Core ML organization, which is responsible for the foundational technologies behind Google's AI. I led the Core ML Foundations team, overseeing our ML Compilers (XLA), runtimes, performance engineering, and the Cloud TPU product. This role was a deep dive into the infrastructure that powers AI at scale.

experience.title: Chief of Staff
experience.description: Served as Chief of Staff for the Core organization, a role I took on to learn how Google is managed as a business—how decisions are made, and how organizations are designed at a macro scale. I partnered deeply with the Senior VP leader, working on organizational design, strategy, and annual planning for a 10,000+ person team.

experience.title: Senior Director of Engineering
experience.description:
I led a number of teams that collect information and build infrastructure to support Google Maps. These teams work together to deliver a useful model of the dynamic physical world that is expressed in a wide variety of geospatial and information products.

Specifically I was responsible for Maps Augmented Reality, Google Earth, Google Street View, 3D and Aerial Imagery, Machine Learning infrastructure to build insights and information from geospatial data assets, and the hardware team that builds the cartographic platforms that support these efforts.

Maps Augmented Reality has recently rolled out features to support walking navigation with unique AR visualizations called Live View.

Google Earth is a well known and well loved product that helps people to visualize the planet and understand how we are all connected.

The Street View program along with the Street View App, Open Spherical Camera API, and Street View ready specs. Collect panoramic imagery from millions of miles of driving. This information is used to support the Street View mode in Google Maps and Google Earth, along with automated map making through our ML infrastructure.

The 3D and Aerial views in Google Maps and Google Earth. These large scale photogrammetry efforts are derived from a fusion of data collected from a variety of remote sensing platforms and our own hardware.

The Machine Learning infrastructure teams work to allow us to drive useful information from the geospatial data we collect from our platforms. This data contributes to keeping Google Maps up to date.

The hardware team builds the cartographic platforms to collect data at ground level (Street View) and from the Air. This data is used to create the Street View application in Google Maps, along with our Aerial and 3D data in Maps and Earth. In addition, this data is used in our ML infrastructure.

experience.title: Senior Staff Engineer / Tech Lead Manager
experience.description:
Managed a team of 50 software engineers in Mountain View, Beijing, Seattle, and Zurich to build geographic imagery systems:

Geographic Image Search, lead a team responsible for unifying Geographic Image Search across all Google Maps products (Google Maps, Google Earth, Google Maps for Mobile, Places API, Local Search). 

Street View, I lead a team responsible for developing the serving systems and launch pipelines for Google Street View. During my tenure the team realized a 300% increase in served data, and a 4x increase in user traffic while decreasing user perceived latency. In addition, the team launched innovative features like the time view in Google Maps.

Panoramio, I lead a team responsible for the continued maintenance of the Panoramio photo community.

Google Maps Views, I lead a team responsible for the creation of the Google Maps Views photosphere community.

experience.title: Staff Software Engineer / Tech Lead Manager
experience.description: I lead a team of 17 software engineers to maintain Google's proprietary HR master data system. Lead an effort to select a new hr system to replace the proprietary system. In addition I lead a team that built a new version of Google's performance management system.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer / Tech Lead Manager
experience.description:
As an engineering manager in London, UK, I was responsible for building teams and delivering products on a variety of platforms:

Google Print Ads, a product to bring the efficiencies and tools related to Google's AdWords platform to the newspaper business. Lead a team of 7 engineers who worked on Campaign Planning, demographics and geographic search back ends. 

Google Translate for Mobile Web, as a 20% project I wrote the first mobile version of Google Translate in 2008.

Google AdWords Professionals, I lead a team of 6 engineers to build a new version of Google AdWords Professionals, which was a certification platform for third party AdWords resellers. 

Rich Media Dynamic Ads, I lead a team of 6 engineers to design and build a system that allowed rich media advertisers to control and optimize the content of their flash creatives in a centralize content management system.

experience.title: Test Engineering Manager

Before founding Scaled Inference, Olcan was an engineer in Google Brain, where he worked on efficient inference methods for general-purpose statistical models and task-specific adaptations that can utilize deep learning. Early on at Google Olcan led several core platform projects that now power virtually all major Google services including Web Search. He later developed the on-demand statistical analysis system that powers Google Trends and subsequently spent many years working on web-scale statistical inference systems at Google Research while also studying AI at Stanford (MS) and UC Berkeley (PhD). Prior to joining Google, Olcan built a popular (10M+ pageviews/month) music search engine and a web analytics platform as side projects during his undergraduate studies at Washington University in St. Louis, where he graduated valedictorian with a BS in Computer Science. data.headline: Experiential Production & Operations Expert
data.summary:
Jeremy Rose is the Chief Operating Officer and Co-Founder of Advoc8, an award-winning experiential agency that specializes in the social impact space. We were recently ranked by Adweek as fastest-growing agency in the country, and 2nd fastest growing agency in the world. 

With executive leadership experience for over a decade from Chicago to Washington, D.C., Jeremy has always excelled in bringing the best team to the table in order to build thriving organizations.

Prior to Advoc8, Jeremy was Managing Director for Harbinger Outreach, Vice President of Operations for Simple Relevance, Partner of COR Strategies, and led operations and advance teams for dozens of campaigns across the country.

recommendations.caption: Creative Visionary. Strategic Storyteller. Talent Cultivator. Mover of Needles. I help brands and creative teams achieve award-winning results through experiences, storytelling, mentorship, and collaborative leadership.
recommendations.description:
The amount I’ve learned from Jeremy while working together is immeasurable. He’s a highly skilled mentor and leader; his abilities are evident in everything he touches. Jeremy is intelligent, insightful, and dedicated to improving processes for his team, not just for efficiency’s sake but primarily for his team’s benefit and well-being. He’s a master of having difficult conversations empathetically and productively. He also provides a lot of insight into the workings of projects and the agency, so the team at large has a good understanding of what’s going on, what it means, and a plan of action to move forward productively. I’ve seen the team dynamic his approach creates, and I’d feel lucky to be on any team he’s leading.

Perhaps most importantly, when I first joined ADVOC8, Jeremy took the time to really get to know me. He quickly saw areas where I thrived, moments I needed coaching, and obstacles I admittedly had difficulty navigating. This was a game-changer for me and allowed him to give me mentorship tailored to who I am. The conversations we had over my time at ADVOC8 helped me navigate projects and client/teammate personalities, but also helped me grow as a person, team member, and leader more than I probably even realize. I’m so grateful to have had the opportunity to work with Jeremy. I am a better version of myself from the experience. And I hope to cross paths with him professionally again in the future.

data.headline: Senior Staff Software Engineer - Software Supply Chain at John Deere
data.summary:
By nature, I am a creator and a problem solver. This is evident in my many hobbies, which include woodworking and working on cars, but also in what drives me professionally. I am a pragmatic full-stack engineer striving to deliver solutions that meet user needs.

I am focused on technique over technology because specific technologies vary across projects and evolve quickly. Focusing on foundational principles enables me to pick up new technologies and apply them quickly, without sacrificing important aspects of a solution. XP practices and automation play a key role in maintaining quality in any technology stack. Embracing a one-web mentality and leveraging progressive enhancement ensures the broadest ability to deliver values to end users over time.

My passion for web development has driven me to pursue roles with a front-end focus throughout my career. However, the web is more than just what can be rendered in a browser. APIs are a strong secondary focus of mine as a result of efforts throughout my career to enable the proper responsibilities in the front-end and middle-end layers of a system.

I constantly strive to grow through experimentation and practice. I am a strong believer in the idea that the road to mastery is through deliberate practice. I have been doing the majority of my practice in public and GitHub has been fundamental in that goal.

I can say without exaggeration that Tommaso is one of the smartest people I know.  He is also one of the most efficient and productive.

Project after project my respect for him grew. He is fearless in the face of complexity and excited to charge into the unknown.   From astronaut level abstraction down to instruction level code Tommaso could comprehend the problem before anybody else on the team.

He is a programmer's programmer.  He extracts exquisite joy from pruning crufty legacy code.  He lives to learn.   He writes his own efficient Bézier smoothing algorithms in his free time for fun.   Yet paradoxically he's a great communicator and focused pragmatically on delivering the best product possible. If you have an opportunity to work with Tommaso, recognize your good fortune.

experience.description:
Forged the inception of Visual Studio Copilot. I bridge products, teams and ideas in the AI developer productivity space. I'm responsible for iterating on novel approaches, weaving new and old feature and product areas together, and designing the next categories of AI implementations for developers. I actively shape the future product vision for Visual Studio Copilot, focusing on advancing chat interactions, code generation, and innovative first-party extender capabilities, such as debugging. data.headline: Product Management at Google | Securing Enterprise AI and Identity
data.summary:
I am a Product leader at Google with 20 years of experience in Identity, Security and AI. My focus is on owning and solving complex, large-scale security and trust challenges to enable both business growth and user safety.

My journey at Google began in Cloud, where I led the 0-to-1 launch of an ML-powered security intelligence platform that helps thousands of enterprise customers achieve "least privilege" access at scale. This work was cited by Forrester as a "breathtaking innovation" and proved that applying machine learning could fundamentally simplify cloud security.

I then took on the challenge of Advertiser Identity for Google's multi-billion dollar Ads business. As a key PM on the team, my work was central to resolving a "Code Yellow" for scaled abuse. I helped lead the product strategy for a novel trust-based system that reduced fraudulent account creations by 50%, securing our core revenue engine while enabling strategic initiatives like the "Great Search Shift."

Today, my scope has expanded to the enterprise level. In Google's Core organization, I lead the product vision for Alphabet's next-generation IAM platform, scaling security and usability for our entire 400K workforce. Building on this, my most recent work is focused on one of Google's highest priorities: securing our foundational AI models. I am defining the product strategy for SAIF, a new paradigm to protect our valuable AI models while accelerating research and development.

The bedrock of my experience is a deep, long-standing expertise in Identity and Access Management (IAM). Before Google, I co-founded and led product for an IDaaS company that we grew to a #1 Gartner-ranked market leader. I am a recognized industry expert and have helped shape some of the standards that underpin digital identity today. I thrive on applying this unique blend of Google-honed product leadership and deep security knowledge to the most critical challenges in technology

experience.title: Product Manager, Google Core
experience.description:
Leading product for Alphabet's next-generation Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform, scaling security and productivity for our 400k-person workforce. My primary focus is on one of Google's highest priorities: securing AI models (like Gemini) by designing and launching novel well-lit paths in ML workflows, and controls to enable secure and rapid AI development, protect against insider threats, and enable regulatory compliance.

- Reduced human and machine access to Gemini model weights by XX% by designing "well-lit paths" in the ML workflow and launching policy-based access controls (PBAC).
- Launched Alphabet-wide tooling to dynamically restrict access based on risk events, enabling faster development in critical areas like AI and beyond.
- Pioneering the 0-to-1 product vision & strategy for Agent identity & access controls

experience.title: Product Manager, Google Ads
experience.description:
Owned the product domain for Advertiser Identity, a strategic pillar for Google's multi-billion dollar Ads business. My role spanned from architecting foundational trust and verification systems to helping lead the product response to a "Code Yellow" for scaled abuse that posed a significant financial risk.

- Resolved a billion-dollar abuse crisis by executing a product strategy that reduced fraudulent advertiser accounts by 76%
- Scaled global advertiser identity verification by 3x, covering 96% of spend across 241 regions, enabling strategic initiatives like the "Great Search Shift."
- Architected and launched a 0-to-1, policy-based IAM platform to serve as the foundation for modern access control within the Ads ecosystem.
- Architected a novel trust-based access system that dynamically adjusted permissions based on real-time risk signals, cutting bogus appeals by 78%.

experience.title: Product Manager, Google Cloud
experience.description:
Led the 0-to-1 launch for Google Cloud's Policy Intelligence suite, a novel platform that uses Machine Learning to solve enterprise challenges of safe and secure access at scale. Owned the vision for applying ML to analyze petabyte-scale logs and deliver actionable security insights

- Led product for Cloud IAM, a planet-scale identity and access platform that underpins all of Google Cloud and its entire customer base.
- Drove the product vision that was cited by analysts as a "top-10 strength" for Google Cloud and a "breathtaking innovation," establishing a key market differentiator over competitors.
- Scaled the Recommender product from concept to adoption by thousands of enterprise customers in its first year, safely removing millions of excessive permissions to dramatically reduce their cloud attack surface.
- Unlocked significant enterprise sales growth by designing a critical privileged access management solution that resolved major deal blockers for Fortune 500 customers.
